subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B9.1.1.1 Apply the understanding of place value in solving real life problems involving integers of any size, rounding this to given decimal places and significant figures
indicator_description: Express integers to a given number of significant and decimal places
examplars:
E.g.1. Express integers to a number of significant figures.
(i) 857,386,321
-five significant figures 
-four significant figures
-three significant figures.
E.g.2. Express decimal numbers to a given number of decimal places.
(i) Write 98745.9674 correct to
-three decimal places
-two decimal places
-one decimal place

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B9.1.1.1 Apply the understanding of place value in solving real life problems involving integers of any size, rounding this to given decimal places and significant figures
indicator_description: Use knowledge and understanding of place value to solve real life problems
examplars:
E.g.1. Create and solve a real-life problem or a story problem and write the answer in standard form.
(I) I am a 6-digit number. My first digit is 5 more than the last digit, but 2 less than my second digit...
E.g.2 Create similar real story problems and solve

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B9.1.1.2 Demonstrate an understanding of the relationship between members of the rational number system and solve real life problems involving union and intersection of three sets
indicator_description: Solve problems on relationship between members of the rational number system using knowledge and understanding of the concept of union and intersection of two sets.
examplars:
E.g. 1 Use sets diagrams to show the relationship among the Real numbers namely
-Irrational numbers (QI)
-Rational numbers (Q)
- Integers (Z)
-Whole numbers (W)
-Natural or Counting numbers (N)
E.g. 2 Write the factors of 12 and 15 and represent them on a Venn diagram.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B9.1.1.2 Demonstrate an understanding of the relationship between members of the rational number system and solve real life problems involving union and intersection of three sets
indicator_description: Apply the concept of sets to solve problems on relationship between members of rational number system and solve real life problems involving union and intersection of two sets
examplars:
E.g.1 Create and solve real life problems to show the union and intersection of two sets. 
i. There are 80 farmers in a certain village who grow either maize or beans. Fifty of them grow beans while sixty grow maize...

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B.9.1.2.1 Apply mental mathematics and properties to determine answers for addition and subtraction of basic facts.
indicator_description: Multiply and divide given numbers by powers of 10 including decimals and benchmark fractions
examplars:
E.g.1. Recall multiplication facts up to 144 and related division facts.
E.g.2. Recall decimal names of given benchmark fractions converted to decimals or percentages (and vice versa) 
E.g. 3. Find the product of a given decimal number when it is multiplied by 10, 100, 1000, 1/10 , 1/100, 1/1000, etc.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B.9.1.2.1 Apply mental mathematics and properties to determine answers for addition and subtraction of basic facts.
indicator_description: Demonstrate the ability to determine commutative properties of addition and multiplication.
examplars:
E.g1. Recognise that for any two numbers a and b;
i. a + b = b + a 
 i.e. 25 + 32 = 32 + 25 = 57
ii. a × b = b × a 
i.e. 17 × 8 = 8 × 17 = 136

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B.9.1.2.1 Apply mental mathematics and properties to determine answers for addition and subtraction of basic facts.
indicator_description: Use the associative property of addition and multiplication.
examplars:
E.g1. Recognise that for any three numbers a, b and c;
i. a + (b + c) = (a + b) + c
or a + (b + c) = (a + c) + b
 i.e. 15 + (6 + 9) = (15 + 6) + 9 = 30
ii. (a × b) × c = a × (b × c)
i.e. (12 × 5) × 4 = 12 × (5 × 4) = 240

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B.9.1.2.1 Apply mental mathematics and properties to determine answers for addition and subtraction of basic facts.
indicator_description: Use the distributive property in solving problems.
examplars:
E.g1. Recognise that for any three numbers a, b and c;
i. a × (b + c) = (a × b) + (a × c)
i.e. 5 × (10 + 7) = (5 × 10) + (5 × 7) = 85
ii. a × (b - c) = (a × b) - (a × c)
i.e. 5 × (10 - 7) = (5 × 10) - (5 × 7) = 15

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B9.1.2.2 Apply the understanding of addition, subtraction, multiplication and division of decimal numbers to solve problems, and round answers to given decimal places and significant figures
indicator_description: Solve operations involving addition, subtraction, multiplication and division using word problems.
examplars:
E.g. Create and solve story problems involving a combination of two or more of the basic operations.
i) A trader sells oranges from two baskets, A and B. Basket A contained 85 oranges and she sold 48. She sold 59 oranges from basket B and was left with the same number of oranges as in Basket A...

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B9.1.2.2 Apply the understanding of addition, subtraction, multiplication and division of decimal numbers to solve problems, and round answers to given decimal places and significant figures
indicator_description: Solve word problems involving the four basic operations and round the answers to the nearest two decimal figures or to some significant figures.
examplars:
ii) The price of a jacket is three times that of a shirt. The price of a jacket is GH₵560.65. Mr Mensa bought two of the jackets and four shirts for his twin sons. Calculate the total amount Mr Mensa paid for the items, correct your answer to:
α) two decimal places
β)three significant figures

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B9.1.2.4 Demonstrate understanding of surds as real numbers, the process of adding and subtracting of surds as well as determining (using a calculator) the approximate square root of a number that is not a perfect square.
indicator_description: Identify simple and compound surds.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B9.1.2.4 Demonstrate understanding of surds as real numbers, the process of adding and subtracting of surds as well as determining (using a calculator) the approximate square root of a number that is not a perfect square.
indicator_description: Explain the identities/rules of surds
examplars:
Rule 1
√a × √b = √(a × b)
Rule 2
√(a/b) = √a / √b
Rule 3
a / √b = (a × √b) / (√b × √b) = (a√b) / b
Rule 4
a√c ± b√c = (a ± b)√c
Rule 5
c / (a + √b) = (c × (a - √b)) / ((a + √b)(a - √b))
Rule 6
c / (a - √b) = (c × (a + √b)) / ((a - √b)(a + √b))

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B9.1.2.4 Demonstrate understanding of surds as real numbers, the process of adding and subtracting of surds as well as determining (using a calculator) the approximate square root of a number that is not a perfect square.
indicator_description: Simplify given surds

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B9.1.2.4 Demonstrate understanding of surds as real numbers, the process of adding and subtracting of surds as well as determining (using a calculator) the approximate square root of a number that is not a perfect square.
indicator_description: Approximate the square roots of non-perfect squares with calculators/tables.
examplars:
E.g.1 Square roots of non-perfect squares
i. √2
ii. √5
iii. √12
iv. √30

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B9.1.3.1 Apply the understanding of operations on fractions to solve problems involving fractions of given quantities and round the results to given decimal and significant places
indicator_description: Review fractions and solve problems involving basic operations on fractions
examplars:
E.g. 1. Review the concept of fraction.
i. Shade the fraction of squares in the rectangle that is equal to the shaded portion of the circle. 
ii. Write down 3 fractions equivalent to 2/5
iii. Express the fraction 15/10 in its simplest form
iv. Express 12/5 as a mixed number
v. Express 2 5/9 as an improper fraction
E.g. 2. Review the basic operations on fractions.
i. Adding and subtracting fractions
ii. Multiplying and dividing fractions

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B9.1.3.1 Apply the understanding of operations on fractions to solve problems involving fractions of given quantities and round the results to given decimal and significant places
indicator_description: Add and/or subtract, multiply and/or divide given fractions, using the principle of order of operations including the use of the BODMAS or PEMDAS rule, and apply the understanding of these to solve problems.
examplars:
E.g. 1. Use the order of operations (BODMAS or PEDMAS) to simplify whole number expressions with more than two operations.
E.g. 2. Use the order of operations (BODMAS or PEDMAS) to simplify fractions with more than two operations.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B9.1.3.1 Apply the understanding of operations on fractions to solve problems involving fractions of given quantities and round the results to given decimal and significant places
indicator_description: Review word problems involving basic operations on fractions.
examplars:
E.g. 1. Solve word problems based on fractions.
i. A test is made up of 20 questions, how many questions must you answer correctly to get a score of 80%? 
ii. By what percentage was a television set reduced if it was marked GH₵2,250 and sold for GH₵2,025?
iii. In an election involving two contestants, one candidate claimed 52% of the votes, while the other candidate claimed 2,681 votes. If 5000 people voted, how do you know the election results are invalid?
iv. Esi and Fusena prepared orange drink by mixing orange squash and water...

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B9.1.4.1 Apply the understanding of ratio, rate and proportions to solve problems that involve rates, ratios, and proportional reasoning and use it to solve real world mathematical problems.
indicator_description: Represent proportional relationships by equations.
examplars: E.g.1 If total cost (t) is proportional to the number of items (n) purchased at a constant price (p), the relationship between the total cost and the number of items can be expressed as t = pn.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B9.1.4.1 Apply the understanding of ratio, rate and proportions to solve problems that involve rates, ratios, and proportional reasoning and use it to solve real world mathematical problems.
indicator_description: Use proportional relationships to solve multistep ratio and percent problems, examples: simple interest, tax, discount and commissions, NHIL, depreciation, insurance, etc.
examplars:
E.g.1 Solve problems on simple interest.
E.g.2 Solve problems on tax (VAT).
E.g.3 Solve problems on discount.
E.g.4 Solve problems on commission.
E.g.5 solve problems involving depreciation.
E.g.6 Solve problems involving NHIL.
E.g.7 Solve problems involving insurance.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B9.1.4.1 Apply the understanding of ratio, rate and proportions to solve problems that involve rates, ratios, and proportional reasoning and use it to solve real world mathematical problems.
indicator_description: Use knowledge of rates and proportional reasoning to solve problems involving SSNIT benefits and contributions.
examplars:
E.g.1 Describe the obligations of the employer/employee and the contribution rates.
E.g.2 Calculate employee/employer contributions to SSNIT under Act 766.
E.g.3 Calculate employee/employer contributions to SSNIT under PNDCL 247.
E.g.4 Calculate employee benefits from SSNIT under Act 766.
E.g.5 Calculate employee benefits from SSNIT under PNDCL 247.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B9.1.4.1 Apply the understanding of ratio, rate and proportions to solve problems that involve rates, ratios, and proportional reasoning and use it to solve real world mathematical problems.
indicator_description: Recognise and graph proportional relationships, interpreting the unit rate as the slope of the graph and use these to solve problems.
examplars:
E.g.1The graph below shows the cost of avocados.
i. From the graph, how much does eight avocados cost?
ii. Also, using the graph how much does 15 avocados cost?

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B9.2.1.1 Demonstrate the ability to construct tables of values for pairs of linear relations, graph the relations in a number plane and determine the intersection of the lines to solve simultaneous linear equations.
indicator_description: Construct a table of values for two linear relations and graph the relation
examplars:
E.g.1Construct a table of values for two linear relations and draw the graphs of the relations 
E.g.2 Draw graph for two linear relations.
E.g.3Construct a table of values for two linear relations.
E.g.4 Draw a graph for two linear relations.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B9.2.1.1 Demonstrate the ability to construct tables of values for pairs of linear relations, graph the relations in a number plane and determine the intersection of the lines to solve simultaneous linear equations.
indicator_description: Use graphs of two linear relations to determine subsequent missing elements in ordered pairs of the relation.
examplars: E.g.1 Find the missing elements of ordered pairs on graphs of two linear relations.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B9.2.1.1 Demonstrate the ability to construct tables of values for pairs of linear relations, graph the relations in a number plane and determine the intersection of the lines to solve simultaneous linear equations.
indicator_description: Use graphs to solve equations involving two linear relations.
examplars:
E.g.1 Solve two linear equations simultaneously using the graph shown.
E.g.2 Solve two linear equations simultaneously using the graph.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B9.2.2.1Demonstrate an understanding of (i) change of subject (ii) substituting values to evaluate expressions, and (iii) factorize expressions that have simple binomial as a factor.
indicator_description: Perform change of subject of a given formula and use it to solve problems.
examplars:
E.g.1 Perform change of subject for given formulae
E.g.2 Use the concept of change of subject to solve problems involving formulae

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B9.2.2.1Demonstrate an understanding of (i) change of subject (ii) substituting values to evaluate expressions, and (iii) factorize expressions that have simple binomial as a factor.
indicator_description: Substitute values into given formulae to evaluate it and use it to solve problems.
examplars:
E.g.1
i. Find the value of (x − b)2 – 3(x − b) if x = 2 and b = −5
ii. Make k the subject of the formula...
iii. The formula for finding the volume of the shape below is given as 1/3 π r² h.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B9.2.2.1Demonstrate an understanding of (i) change of subject (ii) substituting values to evaluate expressions, and (iii) factorize expressions that have simple binomial as a factor.
indicator_description: Factorise expressions that have simple binomial.
examplars:
E.g. Factorise the following expressions.
i. 3x + 4xy = x (3 +4y)
ii. 12ab + 16b = 4b (3a + 4)
iii. -13xy + 39x= -13x(y-3)
iv. 5y-2y2+3y=-3y+3y 
v. 8y-2y2= 2y(4-y)
vi. -6x+12=-3(2x-4)

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B9.2.2.1Demonstrate an understanding of (i) change of subject (ii) substituting values to evaluate expressions, and (iii) factorize expressions that have simple binomial as a factor.
indicator_description: Use the knowledge of simplifying and factorising expressions to solve real world problems.
examplars:
E.g.1 You purchased 10 items from a shopping plaza, and now you need plastic bags to carry them home...
E.g.2 You have to buy two dozen of eggs priced at GH₵10...
E.g.3 The area of a rectangle is 72 cm2. The length is twice its width.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B9.3.1.1 Apply the properties of angles at a point, angles on a straight line, vertically opposite angles, corresponding, angles to` solve problems.
indicator_description: Derive the formula for calculating the sum of angles in any polygon and use this to calculate the value of missing angles in polygons
examplars:
E.g.1. Identify and name the various polygons such as a triangle, quadrilaterals, pentagons, and hexagons, etc.
E.g.2. Derive and use the formula (n - 2) × 180 ° and calculate the value of x (interior and angles of a triangle).
E.g.3. Derive and use the formula (n - 2) × 180 ° and calculate the interior angles of a quadrilateral.
E.g.4. Derive and use the formula (n - 2) × 180 ° and calculate the interior angles of polygons, pentagons, hexagons, etc.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B9.3.1.1 Apply the properties of angles at a point, angles on a straight line, vertically opposite angles, corresponding, angles to` solve problems.
indicator_description: Identify similar and congruent triangles and use the knowledge to solve related problems

E.g.1. Recognise similar triangles and solve for the values of the indicated angles in the diagram below:
E.g.2. Recognise congruent triangles and solve for the values of the indicated angles in the diagram below:

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B9.3.1.2 Construct inscribed and circumscribed triangles and parallelograms with given dimensions.
indicator_description: Draw inscribed and circumscribed circles for triangles under given conditions
examplars:
E.g.1: Use a pair of compasses and a ruler to construct a triangle (say ∆ABC) under a given condition and locate the incentre of the triangle...
E.g..2: Use a pair of compasses and a ruler to construct a triangle (say ABC) under a given condition, bisect at least any two angles...
E.g.3: Construct a triangle (say ABC); bisect all three sides...
E.g.4: Perform a geometric construction of a triangle (say ABC) under a given condition; bisect at least any two sides...

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B9.3.1.2 Construct inscribed and circumscribed triangles and parallelograms with given dimensions.
indicator_description: Construct parallelograms (i.e. square, rectangle, rhombus) under given conditions.
examplars:
E.g.1: Perform geometric construction of a square with a given side.
E.g.2: Construct a square ABCD with AB=6.5cm. Measure and record the diagonal of the square.
E.g.3: Perform geometric construction of a square with a given diagonal.
E.g.4: Perform geometric construction of a rectangle with given side.
E.g.5: Perform geometric construction of a rectangle with a given side and diagonal.
E.g.6: Perform geometric construction of a parallelogram with given sides and given angle(s). 
E.g.7: Perform geometric construction of regular compound plane shapes with given sides and angles.

indicator_description: Use the net of a triangular prism to determine its surface area.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B.9.3.2.1 Derive the formulas for determining the surface area of prisms (i.e. cuboid and triangular prism) and use to solve problems.
indicator_description: Express points in the Cartesian plane as position vectors

subject: Mathematics

indicator_description: Apply the triangular and parallelogram laws of addition to resolve vectors
examplars:
E.g.1 Deduce the triangle law of vector addition.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B9.3.3.1 Demonstrate understanding of how to perform an enlargement on a geometrical shape given a scale factor and describe the properties of the image under the transformation (i.e. congruence, similarity, etc.)
indicator_description: Know examples of situations in everyday life that depict enlargement situations in everyday life.
examplars: E.g. 1. Know examples of situations that relate to enlargement situations in everyday life and the nature of movements – vertical and horizontal.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B9.3.3.1 Demonstrate understanding of how to perform an enlargement on a geometrical shape given a scale factor and describe the properties of the image under the transformation (i.e. congruence, similarity, etc.)
indicator_description: Understand enlargement and identify real-life situations involving enlargement.
examplars:
E.g. 1. Draw an enlargement of shapes using a given scale factor.
E.g. 2. Using an object, and its image, determine the scale factor in a transformation?

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B9.3.3.1 Demonstrate understanding of how to perform an enlargement on a geometrical shape given a scale factor and describe the properties of the image under the transformation (i.e. congruence, similarity, etc.)
indicator_description: Investigate the concept of congruent and similar shapes
examplars: E.g. 1. Using multiple and varied examples of enlargement on a coordinate plane, verify congruent and similar shapes using their properties.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B9.4.1.1Select, justify, and use appropriate methods of collecting data (grouped/ungrouped), use the data to construct and interpret frequency tables and histogram and use it to determine the mode and to solve and/or pose problems.
indicator_description: Identify types of given data including numerical, categorical, ungrouped and grouped data
examplars:
E.g. 1 Discuss, in small groups, information collected in the process of investigation which may be numeric.
E.g. 2 Discuss (in groups) information collected in the process of investigation which may be non-numeric.
E.g. 3 The scores for 11 learners in a class test...

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B9.4.1.1Select, justify, and use appropriate methods of collecting data (grouped/ungrouped), use the data to construct and interpret frequency tables and histogram and use it to determine the mode and to solve and/or pose problems.
indicator_description: Select and justify a method to collect data (quantitative and qualitative) to answer a given question.
examplars:
E. g. 1 Discuss and decide (i) from where/whom to collect data for the studies presented below; (ii) which data collection methods to use; and (iii) justify the choices for (i) and (ii) above
E.g. 2 -Select any study to be undertaken and design an appropriate form to be used in collecting data.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B9.4.1.1Select, justify, and use appropriate methods of collecting data (grouped/ungrouped), use the data to construct and interpret frequency tables and histogram and use it to determine the mode and to solve and/or pose problems.
indicator_description: Organise data (grouped/ungrouped) present it in frequency tables, line graphs, pie graphs, bar graphs and/or pictographs (representations include infographics, waffle diagrams, box and whisker plots and stem and leaf plots) and analyse it to solve and/or pose problems.
examplars:
E.g. 1 -Thirty bulbs were life-tested and their lifespan to the nearest hour are as follows...
E.g. -2 A cleaner of a small office spent GH₵120 of his salary on food...
E.g. -3 – The waffle chart...
E.g. 4 Make a stem and leaf plot...
E.g. 5 – The stem and leaf plot shows the scores...

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B9.4.1.1Select, justify, and use appropriate methods of collecting data (grouped/ungrouped), use the data to construct and interpret frequency tables and histogram and use it to determine the mode and to solve and/or pose problems.
indicator_description: Use a histogram to determine the mode of a given data to solve and/or pose real life cases.
examplars: E.g. 1- The waiting times, x minutes, for 60 patients at a certain clinic are as follows...

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B9.4.1.2 Select, justify, and use appropriate methods of collecting data (quantitative and qualitative), organise and analyse the data (grouped/ungrouped) to interpret the results using the descriptive statistics (measures of central tendency and range).
indicator_description: Select a method for collecting data (quantitative and qualitative), taking into consideration how bias (use of language, ethics, cost, time and timing, privacy or cultural sensitivity) may influence data.
examplars:
E. g. 1 Suppose in a school survey form the following question was asked...
E.g. 2 Ama Mereku in B9 wants to write an article for their school magazine on sport-related injuries...
E.g. 3 Learners in B9 are asked by their physical education teacher to complete a survey...
E.g. 4 - Suppose you tell your classmates that the response to the question in the Class Survey Question Form is to help you plan remedial classes...
E.g. 5 -Suppose in a survey questionnaire you wanted to know the favourite method of cooking pork...

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B9.4.1.2 Select, justify, and use appropriate methods of collecting data (quantitative and qualitative), organise and analyse the data (grouped/ungrouped) to interpret the results using the descriptive statistics (measures of central tendency and range).
indicator_description: Organise and analyse data and interpret the results using the descriptive statistics (i.e. minimum, maximum, measures of central tendency and range) to answer a given question.
examplars: Refer to E.g. 1 of B9.4.1.1.2 and find (minimum, maximum, measures of central tendency and range)

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B9.4.1.2 Select, justify, and use appropriate methods of collecting data (quantitative and qualitative), organise and analyse the data (grouped/ungrouped) to interpret the results using the descriptive statistics (measures of central tendency and range).
indicator_description: Demonstrate the effect on the mean, median, and mode when extreme data is included in a data set
examplars:
E.g.1 Refer to E.g. 1 of B9.4.1.1.2. 
i. Find the mean of the data, if one of the bulbs is replaced with a new bulb with lifespan of 300 hours...
ii. In small groups, find the mean of the data, if the lifespan of one of the bulbs tested was 70 hours...
iii. Continue to replace the values of the lifespan in the data with extreme values...

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B9.4.2.1Identify the sample space for a probability experiment involving two dependent events and express the probabilities of given events as fractions, decimals, percentages and/or ratios to solve problems.
indicator_description: Perform a probability experiment involving two dependent events e.g. drawing coloured bottle tops from a bag without replacement
examplars: E.g. 1In an experiment, Anita was asked to pick one bottle top, in three trials, from a bag which contains 3 red, 2 green and 1 pink bottle tops without replacement.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B9.4.2.1Identify the sample space for a probability experiment involving two dependent events and express the probabilities of given events as fractions, decimals, percentages and/or ratios to solve problems.
indicator_description: Express the probabilities of the events as fractions, decimals, percentages and/or ratios; e.g. using a tree diagram, table or another graphic organiser
examplars:
E.g. 1 Draw a probability tree diagram for the experiment in B9.4.2.1.1...
E.g. 2:
i. Consider the experiment of drawing two Aces (in two trials) in a standard deck of cards without replacement.
E.g. 3:
i. Consider the experiment of drawing an Ace and a Jack (in two trials) in a standard deck of cards without replacement.
